sections.heading: First Sector
sections.body:
Starting on the fairly long main straight, keep an eye on the brake marker boards to the left. Use some of the kerb to the left to extend the track, then jump on the brakes at about the 100 meter board to shift down to fourth gear. Ease back onto the throttle to let your car flow through the Elf chicane that blends into the long Renault right-hander seamlessly while short shifting to fifth.

Renault can be taken flat out - stay to the inside as much as you can and slowly open up the steering as the corners radius becomes bigger.

sections.heading: Second Sector
sections.body:
Brake at about 75 meters for the following Repsol corner, shift to third (or fourth if you prefer a bit more of a planted rear end), let your car get carried to the outside before moving back to the right for the following Seat corner.

There, its down to third. You can apply the throttle earlier than you might expect, but watch out for wheelspin.

Approaching Würth corner, there are no distance marker boards, so look out for the kerb on your right. Brake right where it begins, shift down to fourth, and get back on the power as early as possible for the uphil run to Campsa.

Use as much kerb to the left as the track limits allow for without invalidating your lap, then take a good amount of kerb on the apex - depending on your cars fuel load, you can take the corner full throttle. In a heavy car, a slight lift or tap of the brake might be necessary.

sections.heading: Third Sector
sections.body:
Down to La Caixa on the second longest straight of the track, and you want to brake slightly before the 100 meter board to your right. Move towards the apex under braking as much as you can, going down to third. Patience on the throttle as the exit is quite low-grip - a short shift to fourth can help. Then its up to fifth before a short stab on the brake pedal and a downshift to fourth sees you enter Banc Sabadell.

The kerb with the green astroturf is not your friend - the apex at Europcar is the one behind that with the red astroturf inside.

Coast through this long right hander and dont get on the power too early, as you will understeer and run wide. The penultimate corner, Europcar, can be taken flat out - but dont be fooled by the kerb on the apex of the former chicane. Instead, you want to aim for the one behind that, otherwise you might accidentally cut the track.

Run your left-side wheels on the exit kerb and stay there until turning into New Holland, the final turn. If done right, you can take it flat in seventh, or if you want to play it safe, you can lift slightly and shift down to sixth. Afterwards, its a full-throttle run to the line, and to start another lap.
